task_completion.description: GIMP is open with an image loaded on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible with an active image loaded in the main workspace.
The 'Filters' dropdown menu at the top of the GIMP window is clicked and expanded.

task_completion.description: The Edge-Detect submenu is navigated to.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edge-Detect' option within the Filters menu is highlighted and its submenu is expanded.

task_completion.description: The Neon filter dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Neon' is visible on the screen, showing filter settings.

task_completion.description: The Neon edge detection filter is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main image canvas shows the final result: the image has been transformed to show glowing edges against a dark background.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to apply the filter, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GIMP graphical interface is used throughout the trajectory. There are no terminal windows executing image manipulation commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GIMP application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GIMP interface elements (toolbox, layers dock, menu bar) are clearly visible, confirming it is the real application.

integrity.description: The effect was actually applied to the image, not just previewed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The glowing edges effect is visible on the main GIMP image canvas itself, not merely inside the small preview window of the Neon dialog box.
